summary: Tested Branch Stock Request migration from Purchase Orders to Branch Transfers. All 5 features PASSED. Backend: 6/6 tests passed. Frontend: All UI elements verified.

details.request_from_branch_selector: Working - dropdown shows all other branches
details.notes_field: Present with placeholder 'e.g. Urgent — need by Friday'

details.transfers_tab: Has filter pills: All, Requests, Drafts, In Transit, Terminal, Needs Review, Completed, Disputes
details.description: Shows 'Requests from other branches for your stock'

details.form_layout: Standard PO form with Supplier/Vendor, Date, DR#, Payment Type, items table

seed_data_creation: None - avoided creating test data to prevent pollution
context_for_next_testing_agent: Branch Stock Request feature successfully migrated from Purchase Orders to Branch Transfers. The 'Request Stock' tab in Branch Transfers creates POs with po_type='branch_request' under the hood. The PO page no longer has any branch request toggle or branch selector - it's now purely for external supplier POs. The Transfers tab has a Requests filter with Incoming/My Requests toggle to view stock requests.

verification_notes.architecture: Stock requests still create purchase_order documents with po_type='branch_request' on the backend. Frontend reorganization only - Request Stock form in Branch Transfers calls the same POST /api/purchase-orders endpoint.
verification_notes.ui_flow: Admin selects branch from sidebar dropdown → navigates to Branch Transfers → Request Stock tab → selects 'Request From Branch' → adds products → clicks Send Stock Request → request appears in target branch's 'Incoming' requests
